storeHeader.name: Grandma's Home 外婆家

address.displayAddress: 56 W 22nd St, New York, NY 10010, USA

additionalSections.title: Pricing & Fees
bulletDescriptions: NY law requires that we make the following disclosure because we use information such as your delivery addressto calculate distance and fees, and your past orders and favorite stores to provide personalized promotionsand/or discounts: This price was set by an algorithm using your personal data.
Menu Prices: This merchant sets prices. Those prices may be higher than prices in-store or elsewhere for this location. In-store promotions may not apply. Prices for delivery and pickup may vary.

Service Fee: This fee goes to DoorDash. The service fee may vary but is 15% of your subtotal for most restaurant orders (and 5% for most eligible DashPass restaurant orders). A flat, minimum service fee may apply on small orders. A flat, maximum service fee may apply on larger subtotal orders.
Delivery Fee: The delivery fee is a flat fee that goes to DoorDash. The delivery fee is $0 on eligible DashPass restaurant orders.
Other DoorDash Fees: Long distance fees, express fees, small order fees, and fees in response to local regulations may apply. Each are separate fees, all of which go to DoorDash.
Government Fees: Other fees such as bag fees and bottle fees required by law may apply. Some of these fees may be retained by DoorDash.

Checkout: You can see all of the fees that apply to your order at checkout prior to completing the transaction. The total amount of DoorDash fees on restaurant orders may vary based on the store, its distance, and the order subtotal.

storeHeader.storeBio:
https://a816-health.nyc.gov/ABCEatsRestaurants/#!/Search 

With a goal of warming every guests’ stomach and heart, Grandma’s Home offers authentic Hangzhou flavors from the land of fish and rice (鱼米之乡). 
 Our mission since 1998 is to showcase the homestyle cooking of Hangzhou, along with favorites from throughout the country, in a modern setting.
 With popular locations throughout China, our first American Grandma’s Home brings the same casual approach to regional Chinese cuisine stateside, with a new location in the central Manhattan neighborhood of Flatiron.

address.displayAddress: 56 W 22nd St, New York, NY 10010, USA

consumerReviews.reviewText:
Good package and nice flavor.

Nice value and good quality too.

Pick up was smooth and packaging was neat. I just didntbloke the food., especially mapo tofu. The sauce is too thin and doesnt have the flavor that allows to nameit self "ma". Other chocken dish was ok but more oily than it appears. However, the mango dessert was pretty good .

Dined-in multiple times at this restaurant, it never disappoints! Favourites include: razor clams (please bring this back to your menu!), green tea claypot chicken, crispy fish, hongshaorou, loofah, both soups...actually, everything is very good. Highly recommend! Even after we've moved neighbourhoods, we still come here to dine.
Great food and good value. Loofah melon is fantastic
This is the best Chinese restaurant in New York, in my opinion. Have tied alost everything on the menu, amd everything is great.

popupContent.title: Introducing Store Rewards
popupContent.message:
Select stores now let you earn points towards awesome rewards. Just look for the rewards section on the store’s page to see if they offer rewards.

There are multiple ways to earn points, so review each store’s Rewards section before you order.
